programme.sub-title: The Case of the Singular Double

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.sub-title: Something in the Shadows - Part Two
programme.desc: The identity of the drug-dealer is discovered, and a new student acts strangely.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A man on death row for murder is given a stay of execution amid concerns over the DNA evidence used at his trial - but Horatio remains convinced he is guilty and sets out to find fresh proof. As the case is re-examined, the CSIs receive a call about a new double killing that looks suspiciously like the investigation they are working on.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy reservist is found dead in his home with marks around his neck, and Gibbs and the team discover he was working on a highly classified case for the department of defence. The circumstances surrounding the man's demise lead the investigators to suspect someone was secretly trying to drive him insane, bringing them into contact with Samantha Ryan, the director of a division specialising in psychological warfare. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate the murder of an engineer working on highly sensitive submarine technology, but find the crime scene clean of evidence. Forensic scientist Abby Sciuto travels from the NCIS office in Washington, DC to help out as the case has similarities to a serial killer she has been tracking. Guest starring Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: The crime-busting cleric jeopardises his own life by trying to warn an ageing gang leader that a hitman, disguised as a priest, intends to kill him. Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson star.(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Lavender Lipstick
programme.desc: Perry steps in to help when a chemist is accused of murdering the employer who fired her after evidence is found implicating her in corporate espionage.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a marine working for the Defence Intelligence Agency who was blamed for the disappearance of 500 specialised rifles in Afghanistan. A cryptic message found at the scene, written in the victim's blood, leads the agents on the trail of a controversial manuscript that claims to expose the truth about the missing weapons, and an FBI agent with a secret. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker enlists Trivette's help and cross the border to save one of his former partners from the Mexican mafia. Adventure, starring Chuck Norris.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Naval intelligence ensign is killed in an unusual car accident. Gibbs and his team are reunited with old friend Ned Dorneget, who is now working with NCIS cyber operations, and NSA attorney Jake Malloy to investigate the case, and discover a conspiracy involving terrorist recruitment on the internet.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam investigate when former marine Asher Ross is killed by an explosive device hidden in his mobile phone. Because of the sophisticated nature of the bomb, suspicion falls on the members of the ordnance disposal unit Ross was part of in Iraq, but further enquiries reveal that they are being targeted as well.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents examine the death of a seaman and find themselves working alongside Barrett. Gibbs makes his displeasure about the circumstances of the case well known, prompting the rest of the investigators to worry that he might be about to leave the NCIS again. Meanwhile, Ziva introduces her new boyfriend to her team-mates. Crime drama, guest starring Enrique Murciano.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A bomb goes off in a nightclub, killing the perpetrator in the process. The team examines the body, and discovers traces of radioactive material, sparking suspicion that another device has been planted somewhere in the city. It transpires the bomber was stabbed before the explosion - and Horatio's supposedly dead half-brother could have been involved.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Special Agent Ned Dorneget of the NCIS cyber division joins the team to further their investigation into The Calling, the insidious terror network radicalising youngsters via the internet. When it emerges young suicide bomber Bradley was not, in fact, affiliated with The Calling, Gibbs and his colleagues realise they might be back to square-one. Determined to bring the organisation down, the agents pursue two new leads, an arms dealer who may be supplying The Calling with their signature German S-mines, and another boy given a deadly mission by the terrorists. Guest starring Matt Jones.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A Navy officer dies following a confrontation involving a dancer and a Sudanese emigre at a strip club. However, when investigations reveal he was a code-breaker involved in naval intelligence, Callen and Sam dig deeper and gradually unearth links to Safar, a Libyan-born terrorist known to recruit on behalf of al-Qaeda. Henri Lubatti guest stars.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy lieutenant is found dead in the Pentagon's custodial closet, and surveillance footage indicates she was killed by a co-worker. Meanwhile, suspicions are raised regarding the eyeball left in Tony's drink, and the investigators' attention is fixed on the port-to-port murderer, who appears to still be at large.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: As her trial nears, Yvonne learns the drunk she recently assaulted has been called to give evidence against her, and tearfully tells Tony she is sure the prosecution have enough evidence to put her away for a long time. Amber is paired up with Honey and sent to find the man responsible for two date-rape incidents, but puts herself at risk trying to catch the suspect and almost becomes his latest victim. Phil beats up Steve for sleeping with Kate Maltby, while Zain introduces Smithy to an informant who has had two fingers chopped off by Pete Larson, and warns he runs the risk of losing a more intimate part of his anatomy if he's not careful.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kyla reluctantly agrees to let Justin take Max with him to play football, but the inebriated physio fails to realise the youngster is unwell. programme.title: Casualty

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Drama, starring Kerrie Taylor and Julian Lewis-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey's heating breaks down, leaving them with no one to turn to - except their man-hungry neighbour Dorien. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph.(n)

programme.desc: Beth and Tyler announce they have become vegetarians and set off to Ludlow races to protest about the treatment of animals. Meanwhile, Jed's scarecrow in the barn is mistaken for a human body, and Marlene enrols on a mail-order reiki healing course as part of a plan to create a massage parlour and beauty salon at the farm.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.desc: Compo and Clegg gatecrash Foggy's trip to see a female friend in Wales. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio lament the lack of fresh vegetables available due to a shortage of 'good honest muck' and wonder what it takes to find a half-decent carrot. Whimsical comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Brian Wilde and Bill Owen.(n)

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A young woman's body is found in a seemingly idyllic Northumberland coastal village in 1966, and the subsequent investigation leads Gently and Bacchus to suspect the victim's estranged husband is responsible for the killing. However, they soon come to realise her disturbed family is hiding an even more shocking secret. Starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with Daniel Casey (Midsomer Murders), Shaun Dooley (Married Single Other), Paul Kaye (Two Thousand Acres of Sky) and Greg Hemphill (Still Game).(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The body of a missing computer expert turns up in a hospital morgue under a false name, so the team reopens the investigation into his disappearance. The dead man worked for the Metropolitan Police, giving him access to a great deal of sensitive information - added to which, he was in contact with a group of online hackers. But he died of natural causes - so why was his body hidden, and by who? As the seasoned detectives unravel the mystery, Brian struggles to accept Steve as the new Ucos member, forcing Esther to intervene. Guest starring Sarah Smart and Alex Jennings.(n)

programme.desc: The death of a teenage tennis star occupies the team when it is suggested her suicide jump two years earlier was anything but. programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Richard Poole meets up with a group of old university friends who have arrived on the island for a reunion, only for tragedy to strike when the man who has solved so many murders becomes the victim. Faced with such a sensitive crime, the commissioner quickly drafts in another British inspector to help the team get to the bottom of the mystery. It soon becomes clear that the newcomer, Humphrey Goodman, is just as eccentric as Poole - but he is also just as bright, and wastes no time questioning the partygoers, all of whom are prime suspects, while pondering the biggest mystery of all - how did the culprit commit the crime in full view of all the other guests? Kris Marshall joins the cast, with Ben Miller, Sara Martins and Danny John-Jules.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A zombie movie being filmed on the island ends in real-life horror when a stand-in is poisoned - and new detective inspector Humphrey soon deduces that the unfortunate victim was not the killer's intended target. He believes the culprit was after lead actress Lexi Cunningham, but the murder plot went wrong. The Caribbean cops focus their attention on three suspects - film director Carl Collins, screenwriter Arnold Finch and production assistant Susie Jenkins. But why would any of them want the star dead? Michelle Ryan, Peter Davison and Hannah Tointon guest star.(n)

programme.title: Dame Vera Lynn: We'll Meet Again
programme.desc: In a revised version of a documentary first shown in 2017 to mark the 100th birthday of the 'forces sweetheart', Katie Derham makes use of archive footage and family records to tell the story of how Vera Lynn became one of the UK's most well-known musical stars. Vera marked her centenary by reminiscing about her early days in showbusiness, with help from her daughter Virginia. She recalls starting out as Vera Welch in the 1920s, first appearing on stage at the tender age of seven, before charming the nation's servicemen during the Second World War. She also remembers becoming the first British artist to achieve a number one in America in the 1950s, and a similar feat in 2009, when she became the oldest living singer to reach the same position in the UK charts. Along the way, Second World War veterans and entertainment industry figures - including Paul McCartney, Barry Humphries and Miriam Margolyes - pay tribute to the power of her voice.(n)

programme.title: VE Day 75: The Announcement of Victory
programme.desc: On May 8 1945, Winston Churchill broadcast the announcement that the war in Europe was over. To mark the 75th anniversary of this historic and unforgettable event, Sophie Raworth presents a special tribute to the Second World War generation. Through stirring military music, moving personal testimony and readings from VIPS and celebrities, all those who fought and contributed to the war effort are remembered and celebrated.(n)

programme.title: VE Day 75: The People's Celebration
programme.desc: To mark the 75th anniversary of VE Day, the BBC and the Royal British Legion honour and celebrate the Second World War generation in a programme featuring recollections from some those who remember this historic day. Popular songs of the era are performed by stars including Katherine Jenkins, Adrian Lester, Anton Du Beke, Beverley Knight, Helen George, Sharon D Clarke, Shane Richie and Emma Barton. In a fitting finale at 9.00, the nation is asked to unite for a very special rendition of We'll Meet Again, as a gesture of thanks to the veterans and in memory of lost loved ones. Presented by Sophie Raworth.(n)

programme.title: VE Day: Remembering Victory
programme.desc: May 8, 2015 marked 70 years since Winston Churchill announced the Second World War in Europe was over, prompting Britain to throw the biggest street party the country has ever seen. David Attenborough, Bruce Forsyth, Miriam Margolyes and June Whitfield are just a few of the well-known figures sharing their memories of the end of the conflict. Narrated by Julie Walters, with contributions from others including Johnny Ball, Honor Blackman, Jilly Cooper, John Craven, Cleo Laine, Kenny Lynch, Michael Parkinson, Leslie Phillips, Anne Reid, Esther Rantzen, Patrick Stewart and Una Stubbs. First shown in 2015.(n)

programme.title: Churchill
programme.desc: Martin Gilbert focuses on Winston Churchill's policies from 1943 to the end of the war, a crucial period that saw the Allied invasion of Sicily, D-Day and his second Moscow visit. Always keen to meddle in military strategy, he also engaged in highly sensitive negotiations with the Russians - until forced to acknowledge Britain's economic and military limitations.(n)

programme.title: The Desert Fox (1951)
programme.desc: German commander Rommel falls out of favour with Hitler following his defeat at El Alamein. On returning to his homeland, he is torn between his oath of loyalty and lack of faith in the Fuehrer's unstable leadership, prompting him to become involved in an assassination plot. Second World War drama, starring James Mason, Jessica Tandy, Cedric Hardwicke, Leo G Carroll and Luther Adler.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: Stories of courage, suffering and survival from servicemen and women between 1942 and 1945, as revealed by a generation of people now in their nineties and hundreds. This instalment includes the story of Fergus Anckorn who was once the youngest member of the Magic Circle and - at 96 - is now the oldest. He was taken prisoner by the Japanese in Singapore and put to work on the Burma Railway. He managed to survive using his magic to entertain his captors, who rewarded him with food that kept him and his fellow prisoners alive.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Ruth Hansom, Alex Bond, Samira Effa and Josh Angus compete in the heats for the North East. Each chef prepares a starter and fish course celebrating iconic food from children's literature. This week, the chefs are specifically tasked with honouring the theme of realism in children's literature, as well as heralding books and authors from their region. Dishes include a Secret Garden-inspired starter of potatoes, alliums, egg and caviar, and a Railway Children-inspired creation comprising traditional North East delicacies.(n)

programme.title: Devs
programme.desc: Determined to derail destiny, Lily comes up with a plan - ignore the prediction that she is due at Devs. Meanwhile, Lyndon yearns to return to Devs, but first he must persuade Katie. Later, an unsettled Stewart spouts verse when uncultured Forest encounters him at the entrance to Devs, and as times ticks down to the fixed point Katie told Lily of, Forest seeks solace deep in the past.(n)

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Filmed before the coronavirus crisis swept the nation, the comedian takes on one of his toughest challenges yet, working as a carer. programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Mathematician Hannah Fry and doctor and broadcaster Michael Mosley join an Imperial College, London, experiment to assess the brainpower of the nation, putting members of the public to the test in a battle of wits as they pit young and old, males and females, and tech lovers and readers against one other. The results reveal new science about how people's intelligence changes through their lives, while Hannah and Michael examine groundbreaking new science on how lifestyle and love of technology is changing the human brain.(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Julian Peedle-Calloo and Yvonnne Cobb present from their homes, learning more about the history of video calling and how Covid-19 has affected the deaf community. With voiceover.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: After a frantic race to make it to Ilha Grande, the teams set off for the next checkpoint in Mendoza, Western Argentina, but as the race enters its final phase, the depleted budgets begin to take their toll on the competitors. Emon and Jamiul are the first to set off, but disagree about the best route to take. Jen and Rob are the team with the most money remaining, but also have clashing priorities over how best to proceed. Jo and Sam look for jobs that include bed and board to save their money, while Dom and Lizzie hitch a ride and receive an unexpected invitation.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The penultimate show sees the four amateurs being mentored by Albert Adria, one of the world's greatest pastry chefs. He welcomes the finalists into his Cakes and Bubbles restaurant in London's Café Royal, where he demonstrates two of his dishes, which they have to recreate for a table of world-class pastry experts. After this, the contestants have one final chance to impress, creating one exceptional dish for John Torode and Gregg Wallace that will showcase their growing cooking talent - knowing only three of them will make it through to the final show.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple looking for a home in the Wye Valley, and also examines the challenges of sustainably sourcing and restocking the European eel in the River Wye.(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers get to grips with a protein shake made from dried and powdered crickets, washable cleaning cloths, and an app for locating children that have wandered off.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce pays a return visit to Castle Ward in Co Down, delving into the story of scientist, author and astronomer Mary Ward, who lived in the mansion in the 19th century. Items assessed by the team include two sculptures made out of ostrich eggs and a watch designed for astronauts to wear on the Moon, while military expert Robert Tilney unearths some top secret D-Day plans.(n)

programme.title: Home Front Heroes
programme.desc: Blue Peter presenter Radzi Chinyanganya finds out how his grandmother worked as a land girl during the Second World War and also learns about the realities of rationing.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: Actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents another selection of recipes from TV chefs, including the likes of James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo.(n)

programme.title: Churchill
programme.desc: Profile of Britain's great wartime leader, recounting events from his finest hour on VE Day to the stroke that signalled the end of his political career in 1955.(n)

programme.title: The Wooden Horse (1950)
programme.desc: British PoWs in a German camp try to escape their captors by taking up gymnastics, and using a vaulting horse to conceal the tunnel they are digging. However, even if they manage to escape the camp, the journey to neutral territory will not be an easy one. Fact-based Second World War drama, starring Leo Genn, David Tomlinson, Anthony Steel and David Greene.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team visits the University of Nottingham, where antique expert Caroline Hawley is wowed by a huge Shakespearean-themed quilt and Charles Hanson comes across an 18th-century handwritten Valentine that is hotly contested at the auction. Presenter Paul Martin visits a gorge in north Nottinghamshire called Creswell Crags, where in 2003 prehistoric cave art was discovered.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedian Sara Pascoe, news anchor Clive Myrie, and TV presenters Angela Scanlon and Rick Edwards continue the daily test of their general knowledge skills. Hosted by Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: A look at individuals who played their part in creating a more culturally diverse post-war Britain, including pioneering actor Earl Cameron, who broke through the colour bar in the film industry. Other well-known names also considered are gay rights activist George Montague, and Brian Rix, who has campaigned passionately on the issue of disability. Plus, Arctic Convoy survivor Austin Byrne and former prisoner of war Jim Purcell share their stories and memories of the era.(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary discusses the power of poetry in a time of pandemic, with Game of Thrones actress Emilia Clarke, poet Lemn Sissay and historian David Olusoga. Plus, poetry performances from Helen Mirren and the so-called 'Poet Laureate of Twitter', Brian Bilston.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Susan Calman presents as the chefs from the North East serve up their mains and desserts inspired by children's literature. One chef serves up a dish using oriental flavours inspired by the Chinese takeaway-loving title character in Skellig, while another creates a pork dish in honour of Smashing Saxons. The desserts see a pudding inspired by Jane Elson's A Room Full of Chocolate featuring a delicate chocolate egg, a second take on Skellig - this one using Newcastle Brown Ale ice-cream - and a dessert inspired by The Secret Garden, featuring Yorkshire rhubarb.(n)

programme.title: Devs
programme.desc: With the future obscured and her life in peril, Lily faces a final showdown as she goes through events already witnessed by Katie and Forest - the latter of whom appears to have a Messiah complex. Is Lily equal to defying him so she can prevent her predestined death? Katie, meanwhile, struggles with the emotional responsibility placed on her as tough decisions have to be made. Conclusion of the thought-provoking hi-tech thriller, starring Sonoya Mizuno, Nick Offerman and Alison Pill.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Ardal O'Hanlon plays host in this edition of the comedy showcase, recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. The show features sets by Mawaan Rizwan and Sara Barron.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Big Animal Surgery
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin visits a sanctuary in Sierra Leone to witness a life-saving operation on a young chimpanzee. The animal has a rapidly growing lump on its abdomen and its keepers are worried it is a cancerous tumour. A leading vet uses techniques developed in the world of human medicine to diagnose and remove the lump. But the story takes a dangerous twist.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: After an arduous eight-week journey, the search for the country's best amateur cook reaches its climax as the final three contenders push themselves to the limit for one last time before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace. This is their very last chance to pull out all the stops, show everything they've learned along the way and demonstrate the kind of cook they have become. They must produce the best three dishes of their lives, push their culinary boundaries and deliver jaw-droppingly good food as they vie to lift the trophy and become the 16th MasterChef champion.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: The bikers ride across the Balearic islands Menorca and Majorca, sampling great produce and finding out about the British influence in the Mediterranean from the 17th century to the present day. They reveal how mayonnaise was invented in Menorca's capital of Mahon, sample Menorcan gin, and meet British chef Mark Fosh, who has lived in Majorca for more than 20 years. Recipes include cuttlefish stew and squid salad with mayonnaise, fish and chips in gin and tonic batter, and sea bass a la mallorquina and saffron potato puree.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a vivacious mother and daughter double act hunt for a country home in Norfolk, with a budget of £335,000 budget. There are tears plus lots of laughter as they tour a selection of top East Anglian properties. Away from the house hunt, Jules follows a paper trail to uncover an endangered decorative heritage craft.(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: The makers of an acne-treatment product, a family card game and a sport support top designed to prevent injury during exercise watch on while their items are consumer-tested.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a soup factory in Wigan that produces 2.5 million cans a day, and follows the production of a tin of vegetable soup from a pea harvest to the eventual dispatch. He also encounters a pressure cooker that stands 27.4 metres tall. Cherry Healey compares the vitamin content of fresh and frozen vegetables, and visits a salt mine in Crewe, while historian Ruth Goodman cooks up a butterted spinach soup using a recipe from a 17th-century cookbook, which is the earliest known reference to soup in English.(n)

programme.title: Home Front Heroes
programme.desc: Comedian Helen Lederer uncovers the ways in which two of her relatives took on pivotal but highly secretive Second World War roles assisting the British Intelligence Services. As she pieces together how her grandfather listened in on the secrets of imprisoned German officers, she's astonished to find out the true extent of his wartime work. Narrated by Siobhan Finneran.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The Man Who Never Was (1956)
programme.desc: Allied forces prepare to invade Europe through Sicily - but to throw their adversaries off their scent, they devise an ingenious and complicated plan to make the enemy believe the invasion will happen somewhere else. Fact-based Second World War drama, starring Clifton Webb, Gloria Grahame, Stephen Boyd, Robert Flemyng and Andre Morell.(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.sub-title: Paul Hayes v John Cameron Auction
programme.desc: In Warrington, Paul Hayes and John Cameron go head to head over a cameo brooch. Paul resorts to sneaky tactics in a bid to hide lots from his rival, but will his competitive strategy pay off?(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.sub-title: Jonty Hearnden vs Danny Sebastian - UK Antiques Fair
programme.desc: Jonty Hearnden and Danny Sebastian go head to head at the Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex. Danny thinks he's on track with a vintage railway sign and Jonty's got his eye on the prize with an antique telescope.(n)

programme.title: Scotland's VE Day: Countdown to Victory
programme.desc: Ahead of the 70th anniversary of VE Day on Friday, another chance to see a 2005 programme in which Scottish veterans of the Second World War reflect on the final week of the conflict. They reveal incredible tales of endurance, compassion and cruelty, including Scottish PoWs held hostage by their Russian 'allies', while at home an exhausted nation awaited the final German surrender.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott and Len Goodman visit east London, where the former Strictly Come Dancing judge returns to his childhood sampling some Jewish delicacies and has his first taste of Vietnamese food. Chef Ainsley heads to the kitchen and cooks a mouth-watering dish inspired by the day's adventures.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Burton Constable Hall in the East Riding of Yorkshire, where antiques expert Jonathan Pratt finds a bronze statue and Catherine Southon enthuses about a silver bowl. Paul also finds out about the life of poet Philip Larkin.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon, Clive Myrie, Sara Pascoe and Rick Edwards compete in Double Points Friday, and their scores are tallied from the week resulting in an overall champion being crowned.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three inspirational farming families are celebrated for their ideas and ingenuity, but only one can be crowned Diversification Farmer of the Year at the Farmers Weekly Awards staged in London. Narrated by Denis Lawson.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef heads across one of the busiest border crossings in the world - San Diego to Tijuana - for the start of his Mexican adventures. He follows the Pacific coastline down to Ensenada, and samples flour tortilla burritos, fresh fish, marinated and cooked on Popotla Beach, and visits the Valle de Guadalupe - Mexico's best kept secret for fabulous wines.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The latest two highest-scoring North East chefs cook their six-course menus again to impress judges Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The arbitrators are joined by children's author Jacqueline Wilson, who has written over 100 novels since she started writing books at the age of nine. Presented by Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Rachel de Thame reviews the progress of her new kitchen garden, and begins planting out peas and sowing flower seeds. Nick Bailey shows how to make an easy cold frame for storing precious plants and seedlings, and Joe Swift meets Will Young, whose new-found love of gardening was key to his recovery from mental health problems.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Jason Manford, Jimmy Carr and Victoria Coren Mitchell join regular panellist Alan Davies to answer host Sandi Toksvig's questions on the theme of 'Oddballs'. During the course of the show, the panellists encounter some vegetarian goatsuckers. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the questions lead to points being awarded for the most interesting answers.(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Returning for the final time this season, a self-isolated Nish Kumar hosts Britain's most uncompromising topical satire, tackling all the troubling news from a world that's put its faith in Boris Johnson and Donald Trump. His similarly quarantined correspondents include Rachel Parris, Geoff Norcott and Ellie Taylor.(n)

programme.title: City of Tiny Lights (2016)
programme.desc: A hard-drinking private detective searches for a missing sex worker, leading to a dangerous world of religious fanaticism and political intrigue. London-set crime thriller from Dredd director Pete Travis, starring Riz Ahmed, Billie Piper and James Floyd.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a majestic stand of evergreen trees overlooking sweet tiny flowers growing on a sloping knoll.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Bones
programme.desc: Evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod uncovers the ways that the skeleton aids flight, looking at what makes the humble pigeon a skilled flier and how albatrosses manage to complete long migration journeys. He also explores why some birds have dense bones, and reveals which airborne animal he believes is the 'ultimate'.(n)

programme.title: Michael Wood's Story of England
programme.sub-title: The Great Famine and the Black Death
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores how the great famine and the Black Death affected Kibworth during the 14th century. Delving into the local archives and aided by on-site archaeological excavations, he learns not only that two-thirds of the village's population were lost, but also how these tragic events helped create community spirit.(n)

programme.title: Double Agent: The Eddie Chapman Story
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre revisits the story of wartime double agent Eddie Chapman, the subject matter of his best-selling book Agent Zigzag. He reveals how, during the Second World War, Chapman was able to dupe Nazi Germany so successfully that he was awarded the Iron Cross, the country's most prestigious decoration. Includes footage from an interview the former spy gave three years before his death in 1997.(n)

programme.title: Archaeology: A Secret History
programme.desc: Richard Miles concludes his series charting the history of archaeological breakthroughs by discovering more about the ancient common man. The investigation takes the archaeologist from the elaborate grave of Egyptian boy king Tutankhamun to the Danish resting place of the Tollund Man, who died as part of an Iron Age sacrifice more than 2,000 years ago.(n)

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.sub-title: St Petersburg: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke explore the art and architecture of the Russian city, taking in a selection of imperial, communist and modern-day treasures.(n)

programme.title: Two Types: The Faces of Britain
programme.desc: Mark Ovenden tells the stories of Johnston and Gill Sans, the two modern typefaces that have come to be regarded as the 'faces' of Britain.(n)

programme.title: The Art of Japanese Life
programme.desc: Historian James Fox explores the country's culture, beginning with the connections between Japanese culture and the natural world and the influence of the country's two major religions of Shintoism and Buddhism.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a majestic stand of evergreen trees overlooking sweet tiny flowers growing on a sloping knoll.(n)

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: The critic tells the story of Australian movie-making, focusing on the films that capture this idiosyncratic nation with drama, emotion and humour.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another signature landscape, featuring a clear sky over a welcoming swimming spot, surrounded by shaded trees.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Bones
programme.desc: Evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod explores how skeletons have evolved to help animals sense the world around them, revealing why predators like wolves have their eye sockets at the front of their skulls, whereas many prey creatures have a wider fields of vision. The presenter also looks at the hearing abilities of the grey owl, and another animal's flexible nose.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Andrew Davies' adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel, starring Colin Firth, Jennifer Ehle, Alison Steadman, Benjamin Whitrow and Julia Sawalha. A wealthy eligible bachelor rents a property in Hertfordshire, causing a flurry of anticipation among the ladies of the district. However, it transpires he also brought along a friend - the haughty and aloof Fitzwilliam Darcy.(n)

programme.title: Florence Nightingale
programme.desc: Drama telling the story of one of Britain's greatest heroines, focusing on the aftermath of the Crimean War. Disgusted at the needless suffering of servicemen, the nurse badgered the authorities into allowing her to investigate conditions in military hospitals, little suspecting the effect her findings would have on her own emotional state and the future of her profession. Laura Fraser and Roy Hudd star.(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic dissects his favourite genre, horror, and reveals how film-makers exploit people's darkest fears. Featuring examples from Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, Jordan Peele's surprise blockbuster Get Out, and the movie that has become Mark's lifetime's obsession, William Friedkin's The Exorcist.(n)

programme.title: Science and Islam
programme.desc: Professor Jim Al-Khalili travels to Iran, Syria, Egypt, Tunisia, Spain, and Italy to chart the scientific achievements that occurred in the Islamic world between the eighth and 14th centuries. The nuclear physicist examines the development of algebra by the mathematician Al-Khwarizmi, improvements made to medical diagnosis by Al-Razi and the regular scholarly debates organised by the ruling elite in Baghdad in the eighth and ninth centuries.(n)

programme.title: Novels That Shaped Our World
programme.desc: The documentary explores how novels through the ages have embraced the class struggle, from Dickens, Gaskell and Hardy through to Robert Tressell's The Ragged-Trousered Philanthropists and the group of working-class writers who began to write their own stories in post-war Britain. With readings by Russell Tovey, Darren Boyd, Alex Macqueen, Emily Berrington, Freddie Fox, Rian Gordon and Olivia Barrowclough.(n)

programme.title: Greg Davies: Looking for Kes
programme.desc: The comedian pays tribute to Ken Loach's 1969 adaptation of Barry Hines' classic novel A Kestrel for a Knave. He travels to Barnsley to meet members of Hines' family, including his brother Richard, who was the inspiration for the book's protagonist Billy Casper. He also talks to Loach and Dai Bradley, who played Billy in the film, and talks to Jarvis Cocker about the character's impact on British pop culture.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another signature landscape, featuring a clear sky over a welcoming swimming spot, surrounded by shaded trees.(n)

programme.title: Florence Nightingale
programme.desc: Drama telling the story of one of Britain's greatest heroines, focusing on the aftermath of the Crimean War. Disgusted at the needless suffering of servicemen, the nurse badgered the authorities into allowing her to investigate conditions in military hospitals, little suspecting the effect her findings would have on her own emotional state and the future of her profession. Laura Fraser and Roy Hudd star.(n)

programme.title: Classic Cellists at the BBC
programme.desc: Julian Lloyd Webber examines the BBC archives from the 1950s to the present to celebrate the world of the cello through some of its greatest interpreters. From dazzling performances by legendary masters such as Paul Tortelier, Jacqueline du Pre and Mstislav Rostropovich to some of today's leading interpreters including Yo Yo Ma, Steven Isserlis and Mischa Maisky, the presenter offers a cellist's perspective on an extraordinary virtuoso tradition.(n)

programme.title: Tunes for Tyrants: Music & Power with Suzy Klein
programme.desc: Suzy Klein explores the use, abuse and manipulation of music in the Second World War - from swinging jazz to film soundtracks and from mushy ballads to madcap ballets. The war, she demonstrates, wasn't just a military fight but an ideological battle where both sides used music as a weapon to secure their vision for civilization.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Sybil Ruscoe host the edition first shown on August 10, featuring performances by Shakespears Sister, Jason Donovan, the Dogs d'Amour, Liza Minnelli, Aswad and Big Fun.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s 2
programme.desc: A celebration of disco music, including archive performances by George McCrae, Carl Douglas, Thelma Houston, Sylvester, Eruption, McFadden & Whitehead, Sister Sledge, Gloria Gaynor and the Jacksons.(n)

programme.title: Definitely Dusty
programme.desc: Documentary revisiting the career of Dusty Springfield, the renowned pop diva famous for classic songs such as You Don't Have to Say You Love Me and Son of a Preacher Man. Archive footage shot in both the UK and the US provides an illuminating insight into her glamorous but private personal life, and contributions from her protective inner circle of friends highlight the Dusty behind the panda eyes and blonde beehive. Includes interviews with Tom Jones, Elton John, Burt Bacharach, Lulu and Neil Tennant.(n)

programme.title: Dusty Springfield at the BBC
programme.desc: A tribute to the renowned London-born pop diva, featuring BBC archive footage of some of Dusty's most famous performances from 1961 to 1995. The programme also offers a chance to relive the singer's story, from her folk beginnings with the Springfields, through to her Motown homage years and her collaboration with Pet Shop Boys in the late 1980s. Also includes the star's duets with Tom Jones and Mel Torme.(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Bud falls in love with a snazzy new pair of boots, he might face his toughest adventure yet - learning to tie his shoes.(n)

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: When Walter leaves his pampered pet croc Crunchzilla to fend for itself, Dennis and the gang help him to get in touch with his wild side. Walter does his best to prevent them, as he needs to keep the croc tame in the hope of getting back his father's limo keys, which CrunchZilla has accidentally swallowed.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Historical characters and events are explored in sketches and quizzes based on the books by Terry Deary. Comedy talent including Sarah Hadland, Steve Punt and Meera Syal help bring the strange facts, gory battles and crazy inventions to life.(n)

programme.title: Dame Vera Lynn: We'll Meet Again
programme.desc: In a revised version of a documentary first shown in 2017 to mark the 100th birthday of the 'forces sweetheart', Katie Derham makes use of archive footage and family records to tell the story of how Vera Lynn became one of the UK's most well-known musical stars. Vera marked her centenary by reminiscing about her early days in showbusiness, with help from her daughter Virginia. She recalls starting out as Vera Welch in the 1920s, first appearing on stage at the tender age of seven, before charming the nation's servicemen during the Second World War. She also remembers becoming the first British artist to achieve a number one in America in the 1950s, and a similar feat in 2009, when she became the oldest living singer to reach the same position in the UK charts. Along the way, Second World War veterans and entertainment industry figures - including Paul McCartney, Barry Humphries and Miriam Margolyes - pay tribute to the power of her voice.(n)

programme.title: Our Finest Hours
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth reflects on how families, friends and neighbours have pulled together during the coronavirus lockdown, just as they did 80 years ago during the Second World War. As the crisis has worsened, the support for each other - and especially for NHS frontline staff - has strengthened. Different generations have all done their bit to keep themselves and those around then safe and to look after the most vulnerable members of the community.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: VE Day 75: The Announcement of Victory
programme.desc: On May 8 1945, Winston Churchill broadcast the announcement that the war in Europe was over. To mark the 75th anniversary of this historic and unforgettable event, Sophie Raworth presents a special tribute to the Second World War generation. Through stirring military music, moving personal testimony and readings from VIPS and celebrities, all those who fought and contributed to the war effort are remembered and celebrated.(n)

The road trip pit crew leaps into action when smoke appears inside the classic car, and all fingers point to Charles for this minor mishap. Plus, Christina detours to visit Northampton, the shoemaking capital of Britain.(n)

programme.title: Nadiya: Anxiety and Me
programme.desc: Since Nadiya Hussain won The Great British Bake Off in 2015, she's rarely been off TV screens. But behind the scenes Nadiya suffers from extreme anxiety and debilitating panic attacks, which she's had since childhood. For decades, she has kept her anxiety a secret, ashamed to speak out. Now, she sets out to find the cause of her anxiety, exploring the most effective, available treatments, while also having therapy herself, in the hope of managing her anxiety.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest.(n)

programme.title: Dame Vera Lynn: We'll Meet Again
programme.desc: In a revised version of a documentary first shown in 2017 to mark the 100th birthday of the 'forces sweetheart', Katie Derham makes use of archive footage and family records to tell the story of how Vera Lynn became one of the UK's most well-known musical stars. Vera marked her centenary by reminiscing about her early days in showbusiness, with help from her daughter Virginia. She recalls starting out as Vera Welch in the 1920s, first appearing on stage at the tender age of seven, before charming the nation's servicemen during the Second World War. She also remembers becoming the first British artist to achieve a number one in America in the 1950s, and a similar feat in 2009, when she became the oldest living singer to reach the same position in the UK charts. Along the way, Second World War veterans and entertainment industry figures - including Paul McCartney, Barry Humphries and Miriam Margolyes - pay tribute to the power of her voice.(n)

programme.title: Coronavirus: Clap for Our Carers
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS: Homes for Veterans - Part Two
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team continue with their most ambitious project to date, with the assistance of the local community and two royal helpers, Prince William and Prince Harry. The second edition sees them create a home for a veteran, who is a triple amputee after an IED blew up in Afghanistan. They are also aiming to renovate the fronts of the houses on the street and put in new utilities, as well as creating a lasting legacy in the form of a support centre, which will help other veterans find homes and jobs.(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: David Tennant hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedy actor Chris McCausland and broadcaster and journalist Ayesha Hazarika joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events.(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is upset that Trevor has decided to work for a mission project in Africa, and resolves to give him an unforgettable send-off to remind him of the loving family he has in Ireland. Cathy's family counselling session descends into chaos, Buster and Dermot land themselves in trouble because of a circus lion, and neighbour Winnie is delighted that her husband's libido has returned. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll.(n)

programme.title: Our Finest Hours
programme.desc: As the UK faces its biggest challenge since the Second World War, Sophie Raworth looks at how the nation has responded to adversity in the past and in the present. Using a combination of wartime archive and contemporary news footage, each episode looks at the impact of Covid-19 on families, communities and businesses, and compares how the nation has responded to crisis now and then.(n)

programme.title: Our Finest Hours
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth reflects on how families, friends and neighbours have pulled together during the coronavirus lockdown, just as they did 80 years ago during the Second World War. As the crisis has worsened, the support for each other - and especially for NHS frontline staff - has strengthened. Different generations have all done their bit to keep themselves and those around then safe and to look after the most vulnerable members of the community.(n)

programme.title: VE Day 75: The Announcement of Victory
programme.desc: On May 8 1945, Winston Churchill broadcast the announcement that the war in Europe was over. To mark the 75th anniversary of this historic and unforgettable event, Sophie Raworth presents a special tribute to the Second World War generation. Through stirring military music, moving personal testimony and readings from VIPS and celebrities, all those who fought and contributed to the war effort are remembered and celebrated.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle. Christina buys some quirky curios, while Charles splurges on a multitude of items including a Roman-era ring that he titles the Hanson Hoard. The road trip pit crew leaps into action when smoke appears inside the classic car, and all fingers point to Charles for this minor mishap. Plus, Christina detours to visit Northampton, the shoemaking capital of Britain.(n)

Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of properties sold at auction in Gorleston in Norfolk, Dover in Kent and Wolverhampton in the West Midlands.(n)

programme.title: Our Finest Hours
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth reflects on how families, friends and neighbours have pulled together during the coronavirus lockdown, just as they did 80 years ago during the Second World War. As the crisis has worsened, the support for each other - and especially for NHS frontline staff - has strengthened. Different generations have all done their bit to keep themselves and those around then safe and to look after the most vulnerable members of the community.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The red and blue teams head to Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, where assisted by experts Roo Irvine and Tim Weeks, they hope to bag bargains to take to auction to sell at a profit. Presenter Charlie Ross also visits the Grange Musical Collection to see the largest collection of self-playing instruments in the UK.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest encounters a man who intends to solve his father's murder a year earlier with the use of his brand-new invention - a time machine. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to the Welsh Borders to help a couple with a budget of £775,000 find a countryside home to raise their young son and establish a glamping retreat business.(n)

programme.title: VE Day 75: The Announcement of Victory
programme.desc: On May 8 1945, Winston Churchill broadcast the announcement that the war in Europe was over. To mark the 75th anniversary of this historic and unforgettable event, Sophie Raworth presents a special tribute to the Second World War generation. Through stirring military music, moving personal testimony and readings from VIPS and celebrities, all those who fought and contributed to the war effort are remembered and celebrated.(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers get to grips with a protective cover for mobile phones, heated mittens and a frying pan able to pour sauces. Presented by Lucy Alexander.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle. Christina buys some quirky curios, while Charles splurges on a multitude of items including a Roman-era ring that he titles the Hanson Hoard. The road trip pit crew leaps into action when smoke appears inside the classic car, and all fingers point to Charles for this minor mishap. Plus, Christina detours to visit Northampton, the shoemaking capital of Britain.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and guests celebrate the 75th anniversary of VE Day, as the special Bank Holiday commemorating Nazi Germany's unconditional surrender in the Second World War continues.(n)

programme.title: VE Day 75: The People's Celebration
programme.desc: To mark the 75th anniversary of VE Day, the BBC and the Royal British Legion honour and celebrate the Second World War generation in a programme featuring recollections from some those who remember this historic day. Popular songs of the era are performed by stars including Katherine Jenkins, Adrian Lester, Anton Du Beke, Beverley Knight, Helen George, Sharon D Clarke, Shane Richie and Emma Barton. In a fitting finale at 9.00, the nation is asked to unite for a very special rendition of We'll Meet Again, as a gesture of thanks to the veterans and in memory of lost loved ones. Presented by Sophie Raworth.(n)

programme.title: VE Day: Remembering Victory
programme.desc: May 8, 2015 marked 70 years since Winston Churchill announced the Second World War in Europe was over, prompting Britain to throw the biggest street party the country has ever seen. David Attenborough, Bruce Forsyth, Miriam Margolyes and June Whitfield are just a few of the well-known figures sharing their memories of the end of the conflict. Narrated by Julie Walters, with contributions from others including Johnny Ball, Honor Blackman, Jilly Cooper, John Craven, Cleo Laine, Kenny Lynch, Michael Parkinson, Leslie Phillips, Anne Reid, Esther Rantzen, Patrick Stewart and Una Stubbs. First shown in 2015.(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host reviews trending news stories before connecting to star guests via the internet. On his 'virtual sofa' in this edition are Sandra Oh, who plays Eve Polastri in the hit spy thriller Killing Eve, current Queen of Strictly Oti Mabuse, and singer-songwriter Niall Horan, who offers a home performance of his single Black and White. Rob Beckett and Romesh Ranganathan discuss their new TV show Rob & Romesh Vs, and Stanley Tucci talks about new book 'Dear NHS: 100 Stories to Say Thank You'. Plus, the Red Chair feature.(n)

programme.title: Kinky Boots (2005)
programme.desc: A man inherits a struggling shoe factory, but salvation arrives when he comes up with a plan for the business to manufacture specialist footwear for drag artists. However, the stunned workforce has to be won over, and the new designs must prove their worth at a high-profile fashion show. Comedy drama, starring Chiwetel Ejiofor, Joel Edgerton, Nick Frost and Linda Bassett.(n)

Plus, Lau one of Scotland's most respected traditional bands.(n)

programme.title: DIY le Donnie

programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including making clouds in a jar and how to find out which bouncing ball is spinning the fastest. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.sub-title: Gus am Mathan Molach (Gus the Grizzly Bear)
programme.desc: Following the activities of Gus, a three-month-old North American grizzly bear who lives with his mother and brother.(n)

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Crofters in Borve in Barra gather for a visit from vet David Buckland. At Dick Vet School in Edinburgh, Tim Nuttall uses laser surgery to remove tumours from a dog's mouth. Meanwhile, a dog with a sore foot is attended to in Aberdeen.(n)

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: A focus on unusual leisure activities, including dancing elephants and ball fights.(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports.(n)

programme.title: Taigheadais Dùthchail (Rural Housing)
programme.desc: Ewen MacKinnon looks at the housing that emerged after the Second World War, from social housing and kit houses, to eco friendly modern homes.(n)

programme.title: DIY le Donnie
programme.desc: Donnie Macleod visits Rona's home in Glasgow, where her family is living with a badly finished wall that husband Andy tried to plaster after watching an online tutorial. In no time at all, the DIY expert shows them how to build a new, smooth plasterboard wall.(n)

programme.title: Belladrum
programme.desc: Fiona MacKenzie and Niall Iain MacDonald present highlights from the set by Bwani Junction at Tartan Heart Festival at Belladrum.(n)

programme.title: Mach à Seo!
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon visits Tiree, a beautiful island of white sand beaches, fertile machair and big skies. Enjoying a fun-filled weekend on a budget, Ramsay tries to cash by camping - but getting his tent pitched on a blustery day proves quite a challenge for this camping novice. He travels by bike around the famously flat island and spends what's left of his money drinking the local gin and eating lobster.(n)

programme.title: Ameireaga air Film (America in Colour)
programme.desc: Having recovered from the Second World War, the 1950s saw the US on the up. With rare archive and home movies seen in colour for the first time, this is the story of the '50s as never seen it before.(n)

programme.title: Binneas

programme.desc: Iain Glen stars in a short drama adaptation based on Finlay MacNeill's song The Tears Welled.(n)

programme.title: Na Millenni-Gaels
programme.desc: Mia begins her journey to make it to the British National BMX racing championships and Kyle leaves Glasgow for sunny, blue skies and the smell of sea air.(n)

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service.(n)

programme.desc: Starting in south Skye, sisters Kerry and Kirsty MacPhee catch the Kylerhea ferry over to Glenelg before taking on a steep cycle climb up Mam Ratagan and finishing in Shiel Bridge.(n)

programme.title: About Britain: Isle of Skye
programme.desc: In 1954, Richard Dimbleby and a BBC film crew visited the Isle of Skye to record an episode of the early British television series About Britain. Priya is disappointed that she has to attend her father's party over spending time with Al, and Belle frets at the thought of Jamie going on holiday with Andrea.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: An exhausted Yasmeen sits in her police cell while the police search No 6 for evidence, unaware that they are being filmed on Geoff's CCTV. Alya returns from Spain and heads straight to the police station, Gary leaps to Kelly's defence when Dev blames her for heaping so much trouble on Asha, and Adam and Sarah return from their honeymoon.(n)

programme.title: Absolutely India: Mancs in Mumbai
programme.desc: Brothers Ryan, Adam and Scott Thomas embark on a journey to trace their family roots in India, joined by their dad Dougie, whose father was born in Mumbai. However, the trio have never visited the country they descended from. In the second episode, Ryan, Adam and Scott attempt to find their inner zen at a huge meditation hall, and Scott gets a fiery fix-up Indian style by a hairdresser who prefers flames to scissors. Later, a special family meeting leads to showman Dougie hitting the stage in Mumbai.(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets a 10-week-old collie who has been rejected by its mother, and helps to find a home for a mongrel called Hope who has been at Battersea for over 300 days. Elsewhere, Paul has got his work cut out when a family of nine whippets arrive on site, including some three-week-old puppies in need of some love.(n)

programme.title: Isolation Stories
programme.desc: Four short dramas depicting life in lockdown. The worst fears of paranoid, self-absorbed hypochondriac Mike have come true. There is an unstoppable virus that affects us all, and he can catch it just by breathing the same air as other people. As he struggles to cope with his fears during the Covid-19 outbreak, he insists on an online session with his old friend and therapist, Rochelle.(n)

programme.title: Innocent
programme.desc: Alice and Rob's relationship continues to disintegrate as evidence under questioning incriminates them further and the children grow closer to David. Meanwhile, Tom's attempts to save his marriage are destroyed by his subsequent arrest, and forensic evidence throws new light on David's original conviction.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: Policing Britain: Cops in Lockdown - Tonight
programme.desc: As lockdown continues across Britain, Tonight asks what life has been like for the police, tasked with keeping people safe during these unprecedented times. Rachel Younger follows officers from Merseyside Police to see the impact the pandemic is having on their ability to combat crime, and witnesses the particular crimes and behaviours they are encountering due to the lockdown.(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.sub-title: The Martin Lewis Money Show Live
programme.desc: Everyday life has changed dramatically with everyone facing more restrictions - and more uncertainty. Martin Lewis presents another live special in which he and Angellica Bell will be examining how coronavirus could affect finances, and taking viewers' questions on specific issues.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Driving Test
programme.desc: Mollie finds out if she is finally test-ready after 16 years of learning and more than 250 lessons. Hull mum Cheryl is so eager to drive she has already bought a campervan, while 17-year-old boy racer Callum is back behind the wheel with his long-suffering mum, hoping it will be second time lucky. Narrated by Alan Carr.(n)

programme.title: Isolation Stories
programme.desc: A grandad stops off outside his grandkids' window every day to mess about and make them smile. But his son-in-law, still hurting after being left by his wife, does not welcome these daily visits. David Threlfall and Eddie Marsan star in this one-off drama, which also features Eddie's children Blu and Bodhi.(n)

programme.title: Innocent
programme.desc: David's exoneration allows him to regain custody of his children and intensifies the investigation into Rob, Alice and Tom, leading to a critical arrest. But as damaging accusations concerning DI Hudson's relationship with David threaten to derail the case, her determination to deliver justice finally begins to unravel the mystery of who killed Tara. Drama, starring Lee Ingleby, Hermione Norris, Daniel Ryan and Angel Coulby.(n)

programme.desc: Harriet is angry as Will continues to try to make excuses, while matters between Arthur and Archie get out of hand at Rishi's party, and Vanessa continues with her plans. programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Imran watches on as a bewildered and upset Yasmeen is charged, while Paul puts pressure on Billy to chuck Kelly out of the flat after discovering her role in sharing Asha's video. Adam learns that Ali has left the area after pleading guilty to assaulting Gary, and David wakes with a start and realises with horror that he's missed Max's speech.(n)

programme.title: Captain Tom's War
programme.desc: Just a week after his 100th birthday celebrations, NHS fundraiser extraordinaire and national treasure Captain Tom Moore marks another upcoming milestone in this documentary - 75 years since VJ Day and the end of the Second World War later this summer. He looks back on his time in the brutal Burma campaign and honours the soldiers who fought in what has since become known - with so much focus on the European battlefront and on today's VE Day commemoration - as the 'forgotten war'.(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army (2016)
programme.desc: The officious captain of a seaside town's home guard platoon tries to maintain the reputation and morale of his troops as the Second World War draws to a close. He finds himself dealing with the hunt for a German spy as well as a female journalist sent to report on the exploits of the brave but bumbling soldiers. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Toby Jones, Bill Nighy and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: A young woman's murder is murdered in the vicinity of a library and museum owned by a wealthy philanthropist with an interest in global mysticism. It's a complicated investigation, which leads the detectives into a world of mysticism, erotica, drug addiction and tempestuous family dynamics. But as if the case were not enough to contend with, Van der Valk also finds himself fending off office gossip about himself and his newly single colleague Lucienne. Marc Warren stars as the shred Dutch detective, with Maimie McCoy and Luke Allen-Gale.(n)

programme.desc: Just a week after his 100th birthday celebrations, NHS fundraiser extraordinaire and national treasure Captain Tom Moore marks another upcoming milestone in this documentary - 75 years since VJ Day and the end of the Second World War later this summer. He looks back on his time in the brutal Burma campaign and honours the soldiers who fought in what has since become known - with so much focus on the European battlefront and on today's VE Day commemoration - as the 'forgotten war'.(n)

programme.title: The Perfect Man (2005)

When the family travels to New York, Holly is determined to stay in one place and vows to find her mum the ideal partner - even if she has to make him up. However, when the relationship starts to take off, Holly is faced with the impossible task of producing a perfect man to live up to her mother's inflated expectations. Romantic comedy, starring Hilary Duff, Heather Locklear and Chris Noth.(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news.(n)

programme.title: Cats & Dogs: The Revenge of Kitty Galore (2010)
programme.desc: A secret war between cats and dogs is fought under the unsuspecting noses of the human race. When a rogue feline hatches a plan to bring down both sides and their owners, her fellow cats are forced to seek an alliance with their canine enemies to foil her scheme. Comedy sequel, starring Chris O'Donnell, and featuring the voices of James Marsden, Nick Nolte and Bette Midler.(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Pets (2016)
programme.desc: When rivalry leaves them cast adrift on the streets of New York, two bickering hounds must put their differences aside and pull together. Animated adventure from the makers of Despicable Me, featuring the voices of Louis CK, Eric Stonestreet and Kevin Hart.(n)

Family comedy, starring John Candy, Macaulay Culkin, Jean Kelly, Gaby Hoffman, Amy Madigan and Laurie Metcalf.(n)

programme.desc: Fiona decides to move on from Steve. Ken helps Tricia move into Crimea Street so he can move into number one. Alec refuses to pass on Steve's messages to Vicky.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is still fuming about Chris getting married. Kim tells Frank that she is pregnant. Frank overhears Dave and Biff talking about Dave's affair with Kim.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The doctor tells Kim that Frank has suffered liver failure. Sam invites Eric to spend Christmas Day with the Dingles. Chris apologises to his unconscious father in hospital.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason and Dawson try to trace Rosie's whereabouts in Australia, and Mick becomes a suspect when they uncover evidence of domestic violence in his relationship with her. Meanwhile in Brisbane, Carol visits a monastery to find out more about her long-lost brother, but her search leads to a trap when she receives a call from someone claiming to have information. Starring Joe McFadden, Clare Wille and Lisa Kay.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: Stranger at the Gates - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. A handsome man from Denmark arrives at the household in search of work, setting Mariette's heart racing with his dashing good looks, but Charley is not impressed by the visitor's charms. Drama, starring David Jason, Catherine Zeta-Jones and Philip Franks.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: New tenant Ambrose bamboozles Rigsby by claiming to be a mystic, and soon has the other lodgers under a strange spell. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason and Dawson continue their search for Rosie in Australia, and after ruling out Mick as a suspect, turn their attention to a local roadhouse near to where a missing woman's car was found abandoned. The search is on for the bar owner's son when the police discover he has a criminal record. Meanwhile, Carol delivers Nev's sister's baby and is later overjoyed when long-lost brother Danny arrives in town. Starring Joe McFadden and Clare Wille.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim and Dave spend the night together in Frank's bed. Eric struggles to get into the Christmas spirit. Kathy confronts Dave about who he spent the night with.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dave shocks Biff by telling him he loves Kim. Zak's brother Albert turns up at the Dingles. Kim tells Kathy that Dave is the father of her baby.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack and Vera try to sell Cliff's belongings. Alec explodes when Steve asks for his blessing to marry Vicky. Alma doesn't like the idea of Deirdre being Mike's caretaker.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky books the wedding despite Alec's objections. Denise and Ken move into number one. Rita takes Sally and the children on holiday. Don starts divorce proceedings.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason and Dawson continue their search for Rosie in Australia, and after ruling out Mick as a suspect, turn their attention to a local roadhouse near to where a missing woman's car was found abandoned. The search is on for the bar owner's son when the police discover he has a criminal record. Meanwhile, Carol delivers Nev's sister's baby and is later overjoyed when long-lost brother Danny arrives in town. Starring Joe McFadden and Clare Wille.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The inspector and his sidekick DS Hathaway investigate the murder of a talented maths student. The prime suspect is the victim's ex-boyfriend, who is convinced his uncle has set him up, but old case papers belonging to Morse reveal the boy's family is hiding a dark secret. Laurence Fox and Rebecca Front co-star alongside Kevin Whately.(n)

programme.title: Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: The children's ongoing distrust starts to put a strain on Vivien and Mark's relationship. Meanwhile, Natalie struggles deal with her situation with Tony, and Jake turns to Stella for advice. Later, Jake unearths a darkly disturbing secret about Mark's past. Starring Francesca Annis, Imelda Staunton and Stephen Rea.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars.(n)
